steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: "Though" is a parenthetical word inserted after "the Sun's position" but before the rest of the independent clause beginning with "as the eccentricity of Earth's orbit...". Parenthetical "though" needs to be set off with commas on both sides.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "Axial tilt is not the only factor that affects the Sun's [BLANK] as the eccentricity of Earth's orbit..." — "though" is a concessive parenthetical that must be bracketed by commas on both sides.
  • "position, though,": "...the Sun's position, though, as the eccentricity..." — commas on both sides correctly enclose the parenthetical "though"; the main clause flows uninterrupted. ✓
  • "position; though,": "...the Sun's position; though, as the eccentricity..." — semicolon before "though" implies an IC ends there, but "Axial tilt is not the only factor that affects the Sun's position" is followed by a fragment "though, as..." which is not an IC. ✗
  • "position, though": "...the Sun's position, though as the eccentricity..." — opening comma is present but no closing comma after "though"; the parenthetical bleeds into the following clause. ✗
  • "position, though;": "...the Sun's position, though; as the eccentricity..." — semicolon after "though" would require "as the eccentricity..." to be an IC, but it is a dependent clause. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
